seo.title: Building Content Generation with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration for complex content generation tasks, orchestrating specialized agents for research, writing, editing, and optimization. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent delegation loops, hallucinated facts propagating through the crew, and task drift that compromises brand voice and factual accuracy. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through automated verification at each task boundary.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
The typical CrewAI content generation workflow involves a Research Agent gathering facts and sources, a Writer Agent drafting content in the brand voice, an Editor Agent enforcing tone and guidelines, and an SEO Optimizer refining for search performance. Agents delegate tasks sequentially or collaboratively, with outputs passed between roles for iterative improvement. This modular approach scales content production but introduces risks when agents misinterpret objectives or fabricate information.

Vex integrates as task callbacks, verifying outputs against brand consistency, factual accuracy, originality, tone adherence, and SEO compliance before delegation. This prevents error propagation and maintains quality across the crew.

The architecture supports dynamic crew formation based on content complexity, with human-in-the-loop approval for high-risk outputs.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Agents endlessly pass incomplete drafts between roles, stalling content production
risks.mitigation: Vex task timeouts and delegation limits enforce forward progress

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Research Agent fabricates sources that propagate through writing and editing
risks.mitigation: Vex factual verification gates block unverified claims before delegation

risks.name: Task drift
risks.description: Agents reinterpret brand guidelines, producing off-voice content
risks.mitigation: Vex brand voice and tone adherence checks at each task boundary

risks.name: Inconsistent output quality
risks.description: Different agents produce varying SEO optimization and originality levels
risks.mitigation: Vex standardized verification ensures uniform quality across crew roles

implementation.title: CrewAI Content Generation Crew Setup
implementation.description: Define specialized agents and tasks for the content generation pipeline with clear role definitions.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool
from vex_sdk import Vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

researcher = Agent(
    role='Research Specialist',
    goal='Gather accurate facts and sources for {topic}',
    backstory='Expert researcher with access to premium sources',
    tools=[SerperDevTool()],  
    verbose=True
)

writer = Agent(
    role='Brand Copywriter',
    goal='Write engaging content in {brand_voice} for {topic}',
    backstory='10+ years crafting brand-aligned content',
    verbose=True
)

# Define tasks with Vex verification callbacks
task1 = Task(
    description='Research key facts about {topic}',
    agent=researcher,
    context=[],
    outputs={'raw_facts': 'json'}
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer],
    tasks=[task1],
    verbose=2
)

implementation.title: Brand Voice Enforcement Agent
implementation.description: Configure the writer agent with specific brand guidelines and verification needs.
implementation.code:
editor = Agent(
    role='Editorial Director',
    goal='Enforce {brand_name} voice, fact-check, and optimize for SEO',
    backstory='Former {brand_name} editor with strict quality standards',
    verbose=True
)

edit_task = Task(
    description='''
    Review writer output for:
    1. {brand_voice} tone adherence
    2. Factual accuracy from research
    3. SEO keyword optimization
    4. Originality and plagiarism check
    ''',
    agent=editor,
    context=[task1, task2],
    outputs={'final_content': 'str', 'seo_score': 'float'}
)

# Vex verification specs for content
verification_specs = {
    'brand_consistency': {'enabled': True, 'voice': '{brand_voice}'},
    'factual_accuracy': {'enabled': True, 'sources': 'research_facts'},
    'seo_compliance': {'enabled': True, 'target_keywords': '{keywords}'},
    'originality': {'enabled': True, 'min_score': 0.95}
}

implementation.title: SEO-Optimized Content Task
implementation.description: Final optimization task ensures search performance and compliance.
implementation.code:
seo_optimizer = Agent(
    role='SEO Content Strategist',
    goal='Optimize content for search performance while maintaining quality',
    backstory='Google-certified SEO expert with content optimization focus',
    verbose=True
)

seo_task = Task(
    description='''Optimize final draft for:
    - Primary keyword: {primary_keyword}
    - Secondary keywords: {secondary_keywords}
    - Readability score > 65
    - Meta description (155 chars)
    - H1-H3 structure
    ''',
    agent=seo_optimizer,
    context=[edit_task],
    outputs={
        'optimized_content': 'str',
        'meta_description': 'str',
        'seo_score': 'float',
        'readability_score': 'float'
    }
)

# Complete crew
content_crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer, editor, seo_optimizer],
    tasks=[task1, task2, edit_task, seo_task],
    verbose=2
)

implementation.title: Execute Crew with Output Validation
implementation.description: Run the crew and validate final outputs meet quality thresholds.
